title: Key Socceroos cog ruled out with injury ahead of crucial Paraguay game
original_text:
Jacob Italiano has been ruled out of Australia's final group stage game against Paraguay.

Italiano suffered a groin injury at training, with the extent of the injury not yet clear.

The Socceroos play Paraguay at 12pm AEST on Friday, with the win or a draw seeing them through to the knockout stage.

Australia's hopes of finishing second in its World Cup group have taken a hit, with wing-back Jacob Italiano ruled out with injury.

Italiano suffered a groin injury at training this week, with the extent of the injury yet to be determined.

"We'll do some further tests in the coming days, but he won't be available for this game," Socceroos coach Tony Popovic said at his pre-match press conference.

The injury means Popovic is without two right-sided players for the Paraguay game, after Mat Leckie limped out of the USA match.

But despite two enforced changes, Popovic was coy on who he might call on.

"[I'm] always considering all possibilities because we've always shown faith in every player that's here," he said.

"Since we've started this journey, every player that's in our squad knows that he has a chance of playing and that's no different from tomorrow."

Australia has played three different types of opponents during this World Cup, but there was always a plan for the Paraguay match.

The Socceroos have historically struggled against South American opponents, claiming victory in just nine of 50 encounters and recording no wins since 2023.

Since then, they have lost to Ecuador, Argentina, Venezuela and Colombia.

Two of those losses came in late 2025, when Australia played two friendlies in an attempt to expose the players to a different style.

"It's a different type of opposition we've faced so far … [during this World Cup]," Popovic said.

"We wanted to expose the players in November. We debuted many players and a lot of those players are here now.

"Their qualifying in South America is very difficult. They know how to get a result. They showed that in the last match, scoring early, 10 men, and they were really resilient and tough."

Popovic insisted his side would not play for a draw despite needing just a point to progress out of its group in second spot.

"We're going into the game tomorrow to win," he said.

"To be in this position, third game in our hands to go through, it's a great position to be in and the players deserve a lot of credit."

The game also holds some significance for Popovic, who finished his career against Paraguay, a game he even scored in.

He admitted he noticed the full-circle moment when the fixture was announced.

"When I saw that we've got Paraguay, it's a bit personally something a little special," Popovic said.

"It was a special way to end my international career. And then to think that all these years later that I'll be the head coach and we're up against Paraguay is quite special.

"That was a great day and hopefully tomorrow will be a wonderful, wonderful day for Australia against Paraguay once more."
